([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], 20–16) | cup_wins = 1 | cup_losses = 1 | cup2 = Northern Rail Cup | cup2_result = Won Final
([[Barrow Raiders]], 18–34) | cup2_wins = 5 | cup2_losses = 2 | cup2_winners = 2009 Northern Rail Cup | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = [[Steve O'Connor (rugby league)|Steve O'Connor]] | coach_title = Head coach | coach = [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] | captain = [[Mark Smith (rugby league)|Mark Smith]] | stadium = [[Halton Stadium|Stobart Stadium Halton]] | avg_attendance = 3,412 (Excluding Challenge Cup) | high_attendance = | top_tries = [[Paddy Flynn]] (20) | top_goals = [[Tim Hartley]] (56) | top_points = | prev_season = [[2008 Widnes Vikings season|2008]] | next_season = [[2010 Widnes Vikings season|2010]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Widnes Vikings seasons }}The [[Widnes Vikings]] competed in the [[Co-operative Championship]] in 2009. This was their first season under their new head coach[[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] who replaced the outgoing [[Steve McCormack]].{{cite web|title=Cullen handed Vikings job|url=http://www.express.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/88394/Cullen-handed-Vikings-job|website=Express.co.uk|publisher=Express|accessdate=25 August 2017|language=en|date=9 March 2009}} Under Cullen's stewardship, Widnes managed to advance to the final of the [[Northern Rail Cup]] and ultimately won the cup, ensuring the club could apply for [[Super League]] membership in 2012.{{cite web|title=Vikings claim Northern Rail Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8142517.stm|website=news.bbc.co.uk|publisher=BBC|access-date=25 August 2017}} == Table == {{:2009 RFL Championship|showteam=WID}} ==2009 Fixtures and results== {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" width="100%" !
